stories.title: Arkansas — The Only Host Without a WCWS Berth — Fayetteville Regional Preview

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Every regional host this weekend has been to the Women's College World Series. Except one.

Arkansas. Nine straight NCAA Tournaments. Zero WCWS appearances.

That changes in Fayetteville — or it doesn't. Regionals start Friday. 🥎 #Razorbacks #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Courtney Deifel has taken Arkansas to nine straight NCAA Tournaments. She's lost three home super regionals (2021, 2022, 2025).

The only thing missing: Oklahoma City. Fayetteville Regional tips off Friday vs. Fordham. 🥎 #Razorbacks #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: The Last Host Standing: Arkansas Is the Only 2026 Regional Host Without a WCWS Berth

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:

research.facts:
Arkansas — The Only Host Without a WCWS Berth
**Sources:** Whole Hog Sports (wholehogsports.com), Arkansas Democrat-Gazette, NCAA.com selection announcement
- Arkansas is the No. 5 national seed (HIGH — NCAA.com selection show)
- Arkansas hosts the Fayetteville Regional (HIGH)
- Fayetteville Regional field: Arkansas, Washington, South Florida, Fordham (HIGH — multiple sources)
- Arkansas vs. Fordham: Friday May 15, 4:30 PM ET, ESPN+ (HIGH)
- Washington vs. South Florida: Friday May 15, 7:00 PM ET, ESPNU (HIGH)
- Arkansas has made the NCAA Tournament in nine consecutive seasons under head coach Courtney Deifel (HIGH — Whole Hog Sports)
- Arkansas is the ONLY regional host in the 2026 field without a WCWS appearance (HIGH — Whole Hog Sports, confirmed in multiple articles)
- Arkansas was eliminated in home super regionals in 2021, 2022, and 2025 (HIGH — Whole Hog Sports coverage)
- All other 15 regional hosts have at least one WCWS appearance (HIGH — implied by above)
- Arkansas returned 99.7% of their innings pitched from the 2025 team (HIGH — Whole Hog Sports)
- Arkansas averaging 2 runs per game over their last six games — hitting slump entering tournament (HIGH — Whole Hog Sports May 13 article)
- Courtney Deifel's tenure: 17 postseason victories, more than double the program total before she arrived (HIGH — Whole Hog Sports)
**Confidence:** All facts HIGH.

stories.title: Alabama's Pitching Machine — How the No. 1 Seed Crushes Regionals

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Alabama is 67-6 (.918) at Rhoads Stadium in NCAA Regional play. 1.60 ERA. 22 shutouts this season.

The No. 1 seed doesn't just show up to regionals. They put teams away. First pitch Friday vs. USC Upstate. 🥎 #RollTide #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Alabama's all-time regional record: 74-12. That's an .860 winning percentage — across 27 straight NCAA Tournaments.

USC Upstate, Belmont, and SLU are in Tuscaloosa this weekend. Good luck. 🥎 #RollTide #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Why Alabama Is Built to Win in May: The Pitching Machine Behind the No. 1 Seed

research.facts:
Alabama Pitching & Regional Record
**Sources:** Alabama Athletics (rolltide.com), SI.com Tuscaloosa Regional guide, The Cullman Tribune
- Alabama No. 1 national seed (HIGH)
- Alabama record: 49-7 (HIGH — rolltide.com official)
- ERA: 1.60 (2nd-best in country) (HIGH — rolltide.com)
- Shutouts: 22 (HIGH — rolltide.com)
- All-time NCAA Regional record at Rhoads Stadium: 67-6 (.918) (HIGH — rolltide.com)
- Overall all-time NCAA Regional record: 74-12 (.860) (HIGH — rolltide.com)
- 27th consecutive NCAA Tournament appearance (HIGH — rolltide.com)
- 21st consecutive regional hosting / 21st straight top-16 national seed (HIGH)
- Tuscaloosa Regional field: Alabama, USC Upstate (36-21), Belmont (40-11), Southeastern Louisiana (46-14) (HIGH)
- Alabama vs. USC Upstate: Friday May 15, 12 PM CT / 1 PM ET, SEC Network (HIGH)
**Confidence:** All facts HIGH.

stories.title: Megan Grant's Last Chance — Can She Win a Ring Before Her Senior Season Ends?

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Megan Grant set the NCAA softball HR record (38). She won the NCAA basketball title. Now she wants a softball championship.

UCLA is the No. 8 seed. The L.A. Regional starts Friday. This is her last shot. 🥎 #UCLA #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: One Record Broken. One Title Won. Now Megan Grant Wants the Ring That Matters Most.

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Megan Grant / UCLA

#### Image Concept 5 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Type:** imagn
- **Search term:** `Megan Grant UCLA`
- **Caption overlay:** "38 HOME RUNS / NCAA RECORD / HER LAST SHOT AT A RING"
- **Usage:** X post 1:00 PM + Facebook long-form
- **Alt text:** Megan Grant celebrating after hitting home run in UCLA uniform

#### Image Concept 6 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Type:** imagn
- **Search term:** `UCLA Bruins softball`

---

research.facts:
Megan Grant, UCLA L.A. Regional
**Sources:** NCAA.com, UCLA Athletics (uclabruins.com), Washington Post, Just Women's Sports, Detroit News
- UCLA No. 8 national seed (HIGH)
- Megan Grant hit 38 HRs this season — sets all-time NCAA single-season individual HR record (HIGH — NCAA.com official)
- Previous record was 37, set in 1995 by Arizona's Laura Espinoza (HIGH — NCAA.com, multiple confirming sources)
- Grant broke the 31-year-old record (HIGH)
- Record-breaking HR came in 55 games, 17 fewer than Espinoza needed (HIGH — NCAA.com)
- Teammate Jordan Woolery has 33 HRs (HIGH — UCLA Athletics official roster)
- Grant and Woolery are first teammates in NCAA softball history both with 30+ HRs in same season (HIGH — NCAA.com)
- Megan Grant is a senior (HIGH — UCLA Athletics roster)
- Grant also won the 2026 NCAA basketball championship with UCLA (HIGH — Just Women's Sports)
- L.A. Regional at Easton Stadium — 12th straight year UCLA hosts (HIGH — UCLA Athletics)
- L.A. Regional field: UCLA, Cal State Fullerton, South Carolina, Cal Baptist (HIGH — ESPN bracket)
- Record-breaking HR came against Nebraska in Big Ten championship game on May 9 (HIGH — NCAA.com / UCLA Athletics)
**Confidence:** All facts HIGH.

stories.title: Virginia Tech's Fuel — How the Hosting Snub Made the Hokies Dangerous

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Virginia Tech went 46-10. The NCAA said they can't host due to eligibility rules. So the Hokies travel to Baton Rouge as No. 2 seed.

Angry teams are dangerous teams. Every analyst has VT as the top upset pick. 🥎 #Hokies #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Denied a Host Site, Virginia Tech Arrives in Baton Rouge as the Bracket's Most Dangerous Team

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Virginia Tech

research.facts:
Virginia Tech Hosting Ineligibility
**Sources:** TechSideline.com, SI.com (Virginia Tech), FightingGobbler.com, BroBible.com, WDBJ7.com
- Virginia Tech record: 46-10, 18-6 ACC (HIGH — multiple sources)
- Virginia Tech ruled ineligible to host by NCAA due to graduation eligibility criteria (HIGH — NCAA official statement per SI.com)
- Virginia Tech sent to Baton Rouge as No. 2 seed in LSU's Baton Rouge Regional (HIGH)
- Virginia Tech's first game: vs. South Alabama, Friday May 15, 4:00 PM ET, ESPN+ (HIGH)
- Baton Rouge Regional: LSU (No. 16 seed host), Virginia Tech, South Alabama, Akron (HIGH)
- 7th straight regional appearance for Virginia Tech (HIGH — TechSideline.com)
- VT fell to Florida State in ACC Tournament final (HIGH — FightingGobbler.com)
- Multiple analysts predict VT to upset LSU and win Baton Rouge Regional (HIGH — multiple bracket prediction articles)
- VT was in the No. 17–20 range nationally — just outside hosting cutoff (MEDIUM — bracket prediction articles)
**Confidence:** All HIGH except VT's exact national ranking (MEDIUM — inferred from analyst commentary).

stories.title: Nebraska's Homecoming — First Regional at Home Since 2013

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Nebraska won the Big Ten. Earned the No. 4 national seed. And gets to host a regional for the first time since 2013.

Thirteen years in the making. Opener vs. South Dakota, Friday 5:30 PM ET on ESPN+. 🌽 #Huskers #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Nebraska Hosts a Regional for the First Time Since 2013 — And the Wait Has Only Made It Sweeter

research.facts:
Nebraska Lincoln Regional
**Sources:** Nebraska Athletics (huskers.com), Big Ten official (bigten.org), KSNB Local 4
- Nebraska No. 4 national seed (HIGH)
- Won 11th Big Ten championship in program history (HIGH — huskers.com official)
- First time hosting a regional since 2013 (HIGH — huskers.com official)
- Lincoln Regional field: Nebraska, South Dakota, Louisville, Grand Canyon (HIGH)
- Nebraska vs. South Dakota: Friday May 15, 5:30 PM ET / 4:30 PM CT, ESPN+ (HIGH)
- Louisville vs. Grand Canyon: Friday May 15, 3:00 PM CT (HIGH)
- Nebraska won 2026 Big Ten Tournament — defeating UCLA 7-2 in the championship (HIGH — KSNB Local 4, BigTen.org)
- Nebraska heads into tournament on a 21-game winning streak (MEDIUM — mentioned in multiple preview articles; may need single-source verification)
**Confidence:** Most facts HIGH. 21-game win streak is MEDIUM (mentioned across multiple sources but no single authoritative source found — use with caveat).

stories.title: Five Bracket Upsets to Watch in the 2026 Regionals

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Five upsets to watch in the 2026 regionals:

🔥 Virginia Tech over LSU
🔥 Mississippi State over Oregon
🔥 Arizona State over Texas A&M
🔥 Oklahoma State at Nebraska
🔥 Arkansas State — Cinderella pick

Regionals start Friday. 🥎 #CollegeSoftball

research.facts:
Bracket Upset Picks
**Sources:** NCAA.com round-by-round predictions, Marco News, AOL Sports bracket predictions, Whole Hog Sports
- VT over LSU (Baton Rouge) — consensus upset pick (HIGH — multiple analysts)
- Mississippi State over Oregon — analysts cite Peja Goold and Alyssa Faircloth pitching (MEDIUM — NCAA.com prediction)
- Arizona State over Texas A&M — Kenzie Brown cited (MEDIUM — NCAA.com prediction)
- Oklahoma State as upset threat to Nebraska (MEDIUM — round-by-round NCAA prediction)
**Confidence:** Upset picks are analyst opinions (MEDIUM by nature) — appropriate for Tier 3 fan analysis content. Mark as predictions, not facts.
